name: WIMPzilla / superheavy dark matter
summary: Stable particles with mass m_X ~ 10^11 - 10^15 GeV produced gravitationally during the inflation-to-reheating transition by the expanding background acting on vacuum quantum fluctuations of the X field. Produces relic abundance Omega_X ~ 1 quite independently of any non-gravitational interactions, provided m_X is comparable to the inflaton mass. Connects DM to inflationary physics directly.

addresses_puzzles.note: Naturally accommodates non-thermal DM from inflation; relic abundance set by m_X / H_inf ratio rather than freeze-out cross sections. WIMPzilla DM is largely invisible to direct/indirect detection but may show up in ultra-high-energy cosmic-ray spectra if X is unstable.

touches_benchmarks.note: If X is metastable with lifetime ~ 10^22 yr, decays produce ultra-high-energy cosmic rays above the GZK cutoff; UHECR spectra constrain X lifetimes.
WIMPzilla production doesn't directly modify primordial GW spectrum; consistent with current bounds.

new_symmetries: dark Z_2 stabilising X (or anomaly-free global U(1))
introduces.note: X mass and (if any) lifetime; the production mechanism is fixed by inflationary physics.

history: Chung, Kolb, Riotto (1998), 'Superheavy dark matter', Phys. Rev. D 59, 023501, arXiv:hep-ph/9802238. Term 'WIMPzilla' coined by Kolb-Chung-Riotto (1999) AIP Conf. Proc. 484, 91. Phenomenology developed through 2000s in connection with UHECR origin questions.

references.citation: Chung, Kolb, Riotto, 'Superheavy dark matter', Phys. Rev. D 59, 023501 (1999)
